title: philadelphus lewisii var. platyphyllus
intro: Philadelphus lewisii var. platyphyllus, also known as Lewis's mock orange, is a deciduous shrub native to the western United States and Canada. This plant is known for its white, fragrant flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Philadelphus lewisii var. platyphyllus include its ability to relieve digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and calm the nervous system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, including anxiety, insomnia, and menstrual cramps.
